assertion.summary: Feature-owned proof mapping for one fresh live same-session kill-worker run on Mission session `f1061069-8673-41ad-a30e-26eafb2e80dc`. It ties this feature's checked-in Mission-GUI-TEST Electron screenshot chain to the live `droid.kill_worker_session` request, the same session's `worker_failed(reason = "Killed by user") -> mission_paused` missionDir timeline, and same-session resumable continuation evidence afterward.

automated.case: Mission Control timeline distinguishes user kill and daemon failure events
automated.verifies: Mission progress renders `Worker killed by user` followed by `Mission paused`, proving the runtime timeline distinguishes `worker_failed(reason = "Killed by user") -> mission_paused` from daemon failure.

automated.case: kill messaging distinguishes user-killed workers from daemon failures and acknowledges pending kill
automated.verifies: The UI immediately acknowledges Kill Worker with pending feedback, then exposes paused-after-user-kill recovery copy saying the worker was terminated at the user's request, leaving the Mission inspectable and resumable.

automated.case: mission session plumbing preserves agi/orchestrator settings and supports kill_worker_session
automated.verifies: The real JSON-RPC request path sends `droid.kill_worker_session` for the active Mission session and preserves Mission metadata before and after the worker-kill operation.

interactive.evidence: This feature's new flow artifact centers on one fresh Mission-GUI-TEST Electron run for session `f1061069-8673-41ad-a30e-26eafb2e80dc`: `kill-proof-session-config.png`, `kill-proof-live-before-send.png`, `kill-proof-live-proposal.png`, `kill-proof-live-start-permission.png`, `kill-proof-live-running.png`, `kill-proof-live-kill-pending.png`, `kill-proof-live-paused.png`, `kill-proof-live-mission-control.png`, `kill-proof-live-chat-resumable.png`, and `kill-proof-live-continuation-complete.png` all stay on that same selected Mission session from creation through post-kill continuation.

The live diagnostics log for session `f1061069-8673-41ad-a30e-26eafb2e80dc` records the actual `droid.kill_worker_session` request with `workerSessionId=worker-f10eb4b7-05c2-49c2-acd2-b5e194d4eb40`, followed by same-session Mission notifications. This gives direct transport evidence for the live Kill Worker click instead of relying on restored-session reselection or screenshot-only inference.

The fresh Mission Control screenshot for session `f1061069-8673-41ad-a30e-26eafb2e80dc` shows `Paused`, `Worker killed by user`, `Worker: No active worker`, and the deterministic kill-worker feature queue while the same sidebar session remains selected. The paired `kill-proof-live-chat-resumable.png` and `kill-proof-live-continuation-complete.png` screenshots show the same session's composer enabled and the follow-up continuation response in that same session, proving the killed Mission remains inspectable and resumable afterward.

supportingContext: The same live harness missionDir `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/mission-kill-worker-harness/f1061069-8673-41ad-a30e-26eafb2e80dc/progress_log.jsonl` records `worker_selected_feature`, `worker_started`, `worker_failed(reason = "Killed by user")`, and `mission_paused`, and its `state.json` later shows `orchestrator_turn` after the same-session normal-chat continuation. Those files support the live screenshot chain rather than replacing it.

The isolated session record `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/sessions/f1061069-8673-41ad-a30e-26eafb2e80dc.json` keeps the same session id as `missionBaseSessionId`, preserves Mission metadata (`isMission`, `sessionKind`, `interactionMode=agi`, `decompSessionType=orchestrator`, `autonomyLevel=high`), and stores both the initial proof prompt and the same-session continuation prompt under the required Custom `CCH-GPT-5.4` model.
The broader `.factory/validation/mission-verification/feature-owned-proof-followup.json` bundle remains supporting context only and is no longer the owner for VAL-CROSS-005.

proofOwnershipNotes: This file is the new checked-in feature-owned deliverable for `mission-cross-flow-kill-worker-pause-proof`.
It intentionally narrows ownership to VAL-CROSS-005 instead of relying on the superseded combined follow-up proof bundle.
The Mission-GUI-TEST evidence cited here stays on the Electron surface with the required isolated app-data directory and Custom `CCH-GPT-5.4` model selection.
